channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: JD Vance says he was confronted by pro-Ukrainian protesters while walking with toddler daughter
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Saturday he was accosted by pro-Ukrainian protesters who scared his 3-year-old daughter a week after his tense exchange with the Ukrainian president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump shifts tone on Musk as tensions rise with Cabinet
item.description: President Trump is signaling a softer approach to the mass firings of federal government workers led by Elon Musk as cracks emerge between the tech billionaire and Cabinet secretaries running the agencies he’s gutting.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP seeks spending cuts, defense increases to avert shutdown
item.description: The bill's rollout kicks off a crucial stretch for GOP leadership to lock down support ahead of Friday's shutdown deadline with a Trump-backed strategy that has drawn the ire of Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans call for Trump to cut off American Bar Association
item.description: Republican senators signed a scathing letter to the American Bar Association, calling out the organization's alleged political biases against President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans. The motion for emergency relief was filed late Friday in federal court in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House GOP campaign chairman says voters will 'reward us' for the Trump-Musk DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Almost two months into President Donald Trump's second term, the chairman of the House Republican campaign committee is already predicting his party will pick up seats in the midterm elections some 20 months away. Rep. Richard Hudson, R-N.C.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump is forcing a generational shift in GOP foreign policy. Here's how Republicans are responding
item.description: Republicans in Congress have long been intent on countering America’s rivals and spreading U.S. influence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here's what happened during President Donald Trump's 7th week in office
item.description: Trump launched the week telling Americans that his administration would work to take "America’s destiny into our own hands" and vowed that "this will be our greatest era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Agriculture secretary cancels $600K grant for study on menstrual cycles in transgender men
item.description: The Department of Agriculture canceled a $600,000 grant for the study of menstrual cycles in transgender men, Secretary Brooke Rollins announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: We live in time: Daylight Savings and the 'Time Lords' of Congress
item.description: Scuffles over Daylight Saving Time and its legislative ilk have roiled Congress for centuries. Here's how the long, tumultuous battle over control of the clock has unfolded so far.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada lawmaker proposes searchable domestic violence offender database
item.description: A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would create a searchable database to see if someone has multiple domestic violence-related convictions to their name.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin Democrats vent, cry and issue call to action to counter Musk and Trump
item.description: SAUK CITY, Wis. (AP) — They came to vent and to cry and to exhort. The overflow crowd of frustrated Democrats who met this week in a small Wisconsin city's library voiced anger over President Donald Trump, his billionaire adviser Elon Musk and the direction of the country.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration cancels $400M in grants and contracts with Columbia University
item.description: Multiple federal agencies announced Friday that they're canceling grants and contracts because of what the government describes as the Ivy League school’s lackluster efforts to squelch antisemitism on campus. The U of M is one of five colleges under new federal antisemitism investigations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Homeland Security ends collective bargaining agreement with TSA staffers, an attack on worker rights
item.description: A union representing Transportation Security Administration workers calls the Department of Homeland Security's decision to end the collective bargaining agreement with them an “unprovoked attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs executive order on student loan forgiveness program
item.description: As part of the program, those working for the government or a non-profit organization have their loans forgiven.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens of conservative leaders back clean stopgap to avert shutdown
item.description: Dozens of major conservative activist leaders and organizers are urging conservatives in Congress to support a clean continuing resolution (CR) to avert a government shutdown in a memo organized by the Conservative Action Project.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The EU backs a big defense package as Zelenskyy confirms talks with the U.S. next week
item.description: European Union leaders have approved a major spending package on defense, where Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy — a non-EU leader invited to the summit — received a warm welcome.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz, MPR News

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota’s budget situation worsens in the near and long term with possible deficit growing
item.description: Minnesota finance officials reported worsening in the state’s budget picture in the near term and down the road, saying a small fiscal cushion had shrunk and a future potential deficit has grown.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Dems should recognize issue of border security, de Blasio says
item.description: “Democrats need to say, ‘We believe in order, we believe in security, we need a border that makes sense.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump raises doubts about NATO's mutual defense pact
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested the United States may not defend NATO allies that have not contributed enough to defense spending, casting doubt on a core aspect of the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declines to immediately spare USAID contractors from firings
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday declined to immediately spare U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) contractors from mass firings, letting move forward a core part of the Trump administration’s effort to dismantle the agency. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump prepares order dismantling the Education Department
item.description: The draft executive action, obtained by NPR, acknowledges the department and its signature responsibilities were created by Congress, and cannot legally be altered without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Trump plans to revoke legal status of Ukrainians who fled to U.S., sources say
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration is planning to revoke temporary legal status for some 240,000 Ukrainians who fled the conflict with Russia, a senior Trump official and three sources familiar with the matter said, potentially putting them on a fast-track to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, MPR News

item.title: Tariffs not the way Trump should slow fentanyl flow: Bolton
item.description: "American importers pay the tariffs and then do everything they can to pass that cost on to American consumers," John Bolton said.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Klobuchar, Smith call for answers about potential Indian Health Service office lease termination
item.description: U.S. Senators Tina Smith and Amy Klobuchar are pressing for answers regarding the potential termination of the Bemidji Area Indian Health Service’s lease, underlining treaty obligations and concerns for disruptions in health care services.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Trump issues ‘last warning’ to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday issued what he called a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza, directing a sharply worded message after the White House confirmed that he had recently dispatched an envoy for unprecedented direct talks with the militant group.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. DeSantis endorses permanent protections from vaccines mandates in Florida
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis is set to speak in Tampa with his wife and the Florida surgeon general on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More states want to stop taxing groceries
item.description: The number of states imposing sales taxes on groceries has shrunk over the years, and the number may decrease further in the coming months
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis' State of the State: government accountability a key focus
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis gave his state of the state address Tuesday and offered his plan for the year ahead. Members of the legislature, and leaders past and present gathered in Tallahassee for the speech Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
